{
  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is a cross and circle board game.",
      "It is a game for two, three, or four players.",
      "It is a gambling game."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has pieces that move around the board.",
      "It has a board shaped like a symmetrical cross.",
      "It has cowrie shells used as lots.",
      "It has a name derived from the Hindi word paccīs.",
      "It has versions where the largest score is thirty.",
      "It has similar games that have originated around the world.",
      "It has a history identified from the Iron Age.",
      "It has a representation of Shiva and Parvati said to be playing Chaupar.",
      "It has a board identical to pachisi discovered in the Ellora cave system.",
      "It has a mass market board game version called Game of India."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by pieces moving based upon a throw of cowrie shells.",
      "It is characterized by a name meaning 'twenty-five'.",
      "It is characterized by a cruciform playing area.",
      "It is characterized by castle squares where pieces are safe from capture.",
      "It is characterized by a gameplay objective to move all pieces around the board.",
      "It is characterized by grace throws that allow introducing pieces.",
      "It is characterized by captured pieces returning to the Charkoni.",
      "It is characterized by a strategy involving castle squares for safety.",
      "It is characterized by rules to nullify high value throws.",
      "It is characterized by pieces of different colors: black, green, red, and yellow."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for playing a cross and circle board game.",
      "It is used for determining the amount to move players' pieces.",
      "It is used for representing the players' colours.",
      "It is used for starting and finishing the pieces.",
      "It is used for moving pieces around the board.",
      "It is used for deciding the playing order.",
      "It is used for introducing pieces onto the board.",
      "It is used for moving pieces ahead.",
      "It is used for moving pieces down the centre column.",
      "It is used for capturing opponent's pieces."
    ],

      "It consists of beehive-shaped pieces.",
      "It consists of castle squares.",
      "It consists of a central square called the Charkoni.",
      "It consists of four arms divided into three columns of eight squares.",
      "It consists of specially marked castle squares.",
      "It consists of pieces of different colors.",
      "It consists of rules for introducing pieces onto the board.",

      "It is known for its commercial versions like Ludo and Sorry!."

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはクロスアンドサークルボードゲームです。",
      "それは2人、3人、または4人用のゲームです。",
      "それはギャンブルゲームです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それはボード上を移動する駒を持っています。",
      "それは対称的な十字の形をしたボードを持っています。",
      "それはくじとして使われるカウリ貝を持っています。",
      "それはヒンディー語のpaccīsに由来する名前を持っています。",
      "それは最大の得点が30であるバージョンを持っています。",
      "それは世界中で生まれた類似のゲームを持っています。",
      "それは鉄器時代に特定された歴史を持っています。",
      "それはチャウパルをプレイしていると言われるシヴァとパールヴァティーの描写を持っています。",
      "それはエローラ洞窟群で発見されたパチーシと同一のボードを持っています。",
      "それはゲーム・オブ・インディアと呼ばれる大量市場向けボードゲームバージョンを持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それはカウリ貝の投げによって駒が移動するという特徴があります。",
      "それは「25」を意味する名前という特徴があります。",
      "それは十字型のプレイエリアという特徴があります。",
      "それは駒が捕獲から安全なキャッスルスクエアという特徴があります。",
      "それはすべての駒をボード上で移動させるというゲームプレイ目標という特徴があります。",
      "それは駒を導入することを可能にするグレーススローという特徴があります。",
      "それはチャコーニに戻される捕獲された駒という特徴があります。",
      "それは安全のためのキャッスルスクエアを使用する戦略という特徴があります。",
      "それは高い値のスローを無効にするためのルールという特徴があります。",
      "それは黒、緑、赤、黄色の異なる色の駒という特徴があります。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それはクロスアンドサークルボードゲームをプレイするために使用されます。",
      "それはプレイヤーの駒を動かす量を決定するために使用されます。",
      "それはプレイヤーの色を表すために使用されます。",
      "それは駒の開始と終了のために使用されます。",
      "それは駒をボード上で移動させるために使用されます。",
      "それはプレイ順を決定するために使用されます。",
      "それは駒をボード上に導入するために使用されます。",
      "それは駒を前進させるために使用されます。",
      "それは中央の列を駒が下るために使用されます。",
      "それは相手の駒を捕獲するために使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それは古代インドにあります。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [

    ],

IsA: It is a cross and circle board game.
It is a game for two, three, or four players.
HasA: It has pieces that move around the board.
It has a board shaped like a symmetrical cross.
It has cowrie shells used as lots.
It has a name derived from the Hindi word paccīs.
It has versions where the largest score is thirty.
It has similar games that have originated around the world.
It has a history identified from the Iron Age.
It has a representation of Shiva and Parvati said to be playing Chaupar.
It has a board identical to pachisi discovered in the Ellora cave system.
It has a mass market board game version called Game of India.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by pieces moving based upon a throw of cowrie shells.
It is characterized by a name meaning 'twenty-five'.
It is characterized by a cruciform playing area.
It is characterized by castle squares where pieces are safe from capture.
It is characterized by a gameplay objective to move all pieces around the board.
It is characterized by grace throws that allow introducing pieces.
It is characterized by captured pieces returning to the Charkoni.
It is characterized by a strategy involving castle squares for safety.
It is characterized by rules to nullify high value throws.
It is characterized by pieces of different colors: black, green, red, and yellow.
UsedFor: It is used for playing a cross and circle board game.
It is used for determining the amount to move players' pieces.
It is used for representing the players' colours.
It is used for starting and finishing the pieces.
It is used for moving pieces around the board.
It is used for deciding the playing order.
It is used for introducing pieces onto the board.
It is used for moving pieces ahead.
It is used for moving pieces down the centre column.
It is used for capturing opponent's pieces.
LocatedIn: It is located in Ancient India.
BuiltIn: It was built in Ancient India.
PublishedIn: It was published in the ancient text Mahabharata.
ProducedBy: It was produced by the American toy and game company Transogram.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a board shaped like a symmetrical cross.
It consists of six or seven cowrie shells.
It consists of beehive-shaped pieces.
It consists of castle squares.
It consists of a central square called the Charkoni.
It consists of four arms divided into three columns of eight squares.
It consists of specially marked castle squares.
It consists of pieces of different colors.
It consists of rules for introducing pieces onto the board.
It consists of rules for capturing opponent's pieces.
KnownFor: It is known for its name derived from the Hindi word paccīs.
It is known by the name Twenty-Five.
It is known for being played by Akbar.
It is known for its use of cowrie shells.
It is known for its castle squares.
It is known for its gameplay involving capturing opponent's pieces.
It is known for its strategy of using castle squares for safety.
It is known for its connection to the Mahabharata.
It is known for its variations in rules.
It is known for its commercial versions like Ludo and Sorry!.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Ancient India.
It is associated with the Mahabharata.
It is associated with Emperor Akbar.
It is associated with Louis Rousselet.
It is associated with Irving Finkel.
It is associated with Transogram.
It is associated with chaupar.
It is associated with barjis.
It is associated with Parchís.
It is associated with Parqués.
NamedAfter: It is named after the Hindi word paccīs, meaning 'twenty-five'.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Ancient India.
It occurred in the Iron Age.
It occurred in the time of the Wei dynasty.
It occurred in the 6th or 7th century.
It occurred during the Song dynasty.
It occurred during the Painted Grey Ware period.
It occurred in Mathura.
It occurred in Noh.
It occurred in Chandraketugarh.
It occurred in the Ellora cave system.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the game Ashtapada.
Influenced: It influenced the jeu des petits chevaux.
It influenced Mensch ärgere Dich nicht.
